title: [InstCombine] Reduce range of ctpop for non zero argument

tests.source_program:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32) #0

define i32 @ctpop_select_constant_operands(i1 %cond) {
  %v = select i1 %cond, i32 255, i32 127
  %cnt = call i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 %v)
  ret i32 %cnt
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: This uses a select instruction with constant operands rather than lshr or assume patterns. KnownBits analysis determines the exact population count range (7-8 bits) from the select's constant values, which is outside the documented lshr-focused patterns.

alive2.src:

define i32 @ctpop_select_constant_operands(i1 %cond) {

}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @ctpop_select_constant_operands(i1 %cond) {
#0:
  %v = select i1 %cond, i32 255, i32 127
  %cnt = ctpop i32 %v
  ret i32 %cnt
}
=>
define i32 @ctpop_select_constant_operands(i1 %cond) {
#0:
  %cnt = select i1 %cond, i32 8, i32 7
  ret i32 %cnt
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations

tests.source_program:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32) #0

define i32 @ctpop_select_both_nonzero(i32 %a, i32 %b, i1 %cond) {
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i32 1, i32 2
  %cnt = call i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 %sel)
  ret i32 %cnt
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: This tests ctpop on a select instruction where both branches are known non-zero constants. The summary focuses on lshr patterns and power-of-2 detection, not select instructions with constant non-zero branches.

tests.source_program:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32) #0

define i32 @ctpop_select_nonzero_branches(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %cond) {
  %x_nz = or i32 %x, 1
  %y_nz = or i32 %y, 1
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %x_nz, i32 %y_nz
  %cnt = call i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 %sel)
  ret i32 %cnt
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary examples show non-zero from assume/icmp or lshr patterns. This test uses select instruction where both branches are guaranteed non-zero through arithmetic - a control-flow based non-zero propagation pattern not covered in existing tests.

alive2.src:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32) #0

define i32 @ctpop_select_nonzero_branches(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %cond) {
  %x_nz = or i32 %x, 1
  %y_nz = or i32 %y, 1
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %x_nz, i32 %y_nz
  %cnt = call i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 %sel)
  ret i32 %cnt
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

; Function Attrs: nocallback nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32) #0

define i32 @ctpop_select_nonzero_branches(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %cond) {
  %sel.v = select i1 %cond, i32 %x, i32 %y
  %sel = or i32 %sel.v, 1
  %cnt = call range(i32 1, 33) i32 @llvm.ctpop.i32(i32 %sel)
  ret i32 %cnt
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @ctpop_select_nonzero_branches(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %cond) {
#0:
  %x_nz = or i32 %x, 1
  %y_nz = or i32 %y, 1
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %x_nz, i32 %y_nz
  %cnt = ctpop i32 %sel
  ret i32 %cnt
}
=>
define i32 @ctpop_select_nonzero_branches(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %cond) {
#0:
  %sel.v = select i1 %cond, i32 %x, i32 %y
  %sel = or i32 %sel.v, 1
  %cnt = ctpop i32 %sel
  %#range_0_%cnt = !range i32 %cnt, i32 1, i32 33
  ret i32 %#range_0_%cnt
}
Transformation seems to be correct!
